title: Capirona macrophylla
intro: Capirona macrophylla, also known as Capirona or Kapok tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Capirona macrophylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Capirona macrophylla is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as in combination with other plants to create remedies for various health conditions.
